issue_title: Error indexing heiroglyphics into a match_only_text field
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

9.4.0

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

any

### Problem Description

Indexing heiroglyphics into a `match_only_text` errors with
```
Reader returned 0 characters. See JFlex examples/zero-reader for a workaround
``` 

### Steps to Reproduce

1 Create an index with the mapping
```
PUT repro-jflex-bug
{
  "mappings": {
    "properties": {
      "url": {
        "type": "match_only_text"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

2 Use this Python script to index some heiroglyphics
```
import requests, json

#Egyptian hieroglyphs start at Unicode block U+13000
hieroglyphs = "".join(chr(0x13000 + i % 48) for i in range(128))
payload = {"url": f"https://example.com/?q={hieroglyphs}"}

resp = requests.put(
    "http://localhost:9200/repro-jflex-bug/_doc/1",
    headers={
        "Content-Type": "application/json"        
    },
    data=json.dumps(payload)
)

print(resp.json())
```

commit_message:
Fix match only text decoding surrogate pairs (#146567)

In #129371, we added the UTF8DecodingReader as an optimization to improve
indexing throughput. The idea was that during indexing, we could avoid
storing the whole UTF16 decoded string in memory, instead decoding it
on-the-fly as needed.

However, performance gains were negligible, and it has since caused the
issue #146538.

So this PR reverts that change, reverting to just passing lucene the
decoded String.

commit_message:
Ensure stable master at the end of testSeqNoCASLinearizability (#146681)

An induced network partition can produce more than
one round of elections when it is repaired. If
post-test cluster validation runs while this is
happening, it may race with the elections and see an
inconsistent state.

To avoid the race, the test now publishes a trivial cluster
state update before concluding and waits for it to be
successfully processed.

Fixes #146568

issue_body:
Bumped into this while working on an unrelated PR. This is a release test, so you probably don't need to fix it now, but at some point you should.

`PushExpressionToLoadIT.testRoundToLong` and `PushExpressionToLoadIT.testRoundToLongExactMatch` fail in `release-tests` (non-snapshot) builds because the underlying `ROUND_TO_BLOCK_LOADER` capability is gated on `Build.current().isSnapshot()` but the tests assert the optimized path unconditionally.

Introduced by PR #144234 (@not-napoleon). That PR did not carry the `test-release` label, so release-tests never ran against it and the miss shipped to main. The miss surfaced on elastic/elasticsearch#146063 which carries `test-release` for its own FF-gated work.

### Root cause

```java
// x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/esql/action/EsqlCapabilities.java:2386
ROUND_TO_BLOCK_LOADER(Build.current().isSnapshot()),
```

The capability is snapshot-only. In release builds the optimizer falls back to the unoptimized `LongsFromDocValues.Singleton` block loader. The two tests in `PushExpressionToLoadIT` assert `RoundToLongsFromDocValues.Singleton` without guarding on the build type:

```java
// PushExpressionToLoadIT.java:413
public void testRoundToLong() throws IOException {
    ...
    matchesMap().entry("test:column_at_a_time:RoundToLongsFromDocValues.Singleton", 1),
    ...
}

// PushExpressionToLoadIT.java:431
public void testRoundToLongExactMatch() throws IOException {
    ...
    matchesMap().entry("test:column_at_a_time:RoundToLongsFromDocValues.Singleton", 1),
    ...
}
```

### Observed failure

```
java.lang.AssertionError: Error checking values loaded for driver data; Expected a map containing
    operator: "ValuesSourceReaderOperator[fields = [test]]"
    status: a map containing
      readers_built: expected (a map containing
        test:column_at_a_time:RoundToLongsFromDocValues.Singleton: <1>)
      but was <{test:column_at_a_time:LongsFromDocValues.Singleton=1}>
```

Build scan / failing job: https://buildkite.com/elastic/elasticsearch-pull-request/builds/137843#019d9759-ec51-47f4-83ea-55cf301e8e58

### Reproduce

```
./gradlew ":x-pack:plugin:esql:qa:server:single-node:javaRestTest" \
  --tests "org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.qa.single_node.PushExpressionToLoadIT.testRoundToLong" \
  -Dtests.seed=66B2C21DB080217F \
  -Dbuild.snapshot=false \
  -Dtests.jvm.argline="-Dbuild.snapshot=false"
```

Same for `testRoundToLongExactMatch`. The failure is deterministic in release mode — the random seed and locale are not relevant.

### Suggested fix

Add a build-type guard at the top of each test, matching the idiom already used elsewhere in this file (e.g. `PushExpressionToLoadIT.java:1064` uses `Build.current().isSnapshot() ? ... : ...` for a similar snapshot-only optimization):

```java
public void testRoundToLong() throws IOException {
    assumeTrue("ROUND_TO block loader capability is snapshot-only", Build.current().isSnapshot());
    ...
}
```

Alternatively, un-gate `ROUND_TO_BLOCK_LOADER` from snapshot-only if the feature is considered stable.

### Impact

Blocks any PR that carries the `test-release` label and picks up main after 2026-04-15 (e.g., #146063). Does not affect normal snapshot CI, which is why it went undetected until a test-release PR hit main.

issue_title: OOM: phrase query on match_only_text in TSDB index
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

9.3.3

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

Serverless, 4Gb heap of a search node

### Problem Description

Phrase / intervals / positional queries on a `match_only_text` field in a
TSDB index can trigger `OutOfMemoryError` under concurrent search. Because
`match_only_text` does not index positions, these queries fall back to
`SourceConfirmedTextQuery`, which constructs a `RuntimePhraseScorer` per
leaf per positional clause, each capturing a per-leaf value fetcher that
lives for the scorer's lifetime.

On TSDB indices the fetcher resolves to
`AbstractTSDBDocValuesProducer.getCompressedBinary`, which eagerly allocates
a `BinaryDecoder` with a multi-MB `uncompressedBlock` buffer. Under
concurrent search across many shards and segments, hundreds of these
scorers (and decoders) can be live simultaneously, and the retained heap
grows to several GB. Either allocating the `BinaryDecoder`'s buffers
lazily on first `decode(...)`, or deferring the value-fetcher open in
`MatchOnlyTextFieldMapper` until the scorer is actually advanced, would
prevent this.

### Heap analysis

  SourceConfirmedTextQuery$RuntimePhraseScorer  786      3,191,381,288 B retained (90.8%)

commit_message:
Regenerate test certificates (#147819)

Regenerates expired test certificates to fix JDK 26 SSL failures that JDK 26 introduced indirectly with `X509KeyManagerCertChecking`. When an expired certificate is encountered, the validity check calls Date.toString() on the expiry date that fails with `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`.  More info in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146645#issuecomment-4293971360

commit_message:
Fix testReindexFailsWhenPitRelocationFails (#147724)

The .tasks index was waiting a minute before relocating, and this was
causing the assertBusy to time out. This change manually sets the
INDEX_DELAYED_NODE_LEFT_TIMEOUT_SETTING to be zero, forcing the .tasks
index to relocate immediately and removing this race condition

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146650

issue_title: ESQL:DS: ndjson plugin: wire circuit breaking in
issue_body:
### Description

`NdJsonPageIterator#trimLastPartialLine` simply reads into a `ByteArrayOutputStream` the content of a `StorageObject`. This content can get humongous. There's no protection against OOMs here.
This is to wire CBs in and investigate other untracked pressure points.
commit_message:
ESQL:DS: NDJSON: rework trimming last partial line (#147041)

This changes how trimming the last partial line of an NDJSON
chunk (such as that which results from a slice of a decompressed block)
is performed: instead of reading all the stream into a temporary buffer
and trimming the last line, it wraps the InputStream and does the
trimming into the wrap.

This wrapping InputStream will have to keep a buffer of already read,
but not yet delimited input (no LF encountered). This is capped at up to
32 MB (current limit), meaning a NDJSON line can't exceed this size.
If it does, decoding it fails (respecting the strictness of the error policy).

Closes #146667

commit_message:
Test reindex task relocates on node shutdown test failure (#146829)

There was a race condition in the cluster teardown between writing to
the `.tasks` index and shutting down the cluster. The
`testReindexTaskRelocatesOnNodeShutdown` test has now been modified to
use the get tasks api to wait for the task to be fully completed before
initiating teardown

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146696
